Test Scenario: Assessment data and credentials handled securely
Acceptance Criteria: No PII or raw evidence sent to AI; AWS credentials not exposed in UI; file-based storage has appropriate permissions
Steps to Execute: 1. Run assessment; 2. Use AI features; 3. Inspect network requests for data leakage; 4. Check file permissions

Test Scenario: Session state and persistence secure
Acceptance Criteria: Cross-tab data sharing via .sopra_results.json does not expose secrets; Back to Dashboard works without leaking state
Steps to Execute: 1. Open multiple tabs (assessment/remediation/ISSO); 2. Verify data sharing; 3. Check for sensitive data in URLs

Test Scenario: AI-generated content clearly labeled
Acceptance Criteria: All AI output tagged with visible badge; human can distinguish AI vs manual content
Steps to Execute: 1. Use POA&M AI generate; 2. Use Risk Acceptance AI draft; 3. Use evidence analysis; 4. Verify badges present

Test Scenario: Application functions without AI when Bedrock unavailable
Acceptance Criteria: Graceful fallback to templates; no crashes; user informed when AI unavailable
Steps to Execute: 1. Disable Bedrock or use invalid creds; 2. Trigger each AI feature; 3. Verify template fallback

Test Scenario: 200 controls align to NIST 800-53 Rev 5 and CIS v8
Acceptance Criteria: Control definitions and mappings accurate; bidirectional crosswalk correct
Steps to Execute: 1. Open Control Crosswalk; 2. Review NIST and CIS mappings; 3. Spot-check against framework docs

Test Scenario: FIPS 199 baselines filter controls correctly
Acceptance Criteria: Low (75) Moderate (155) High (200) baselines applied; filtering changes control set as expected
Steps to Execute: 1. Select Low baseline; 2. Count controls; 3. Switch to Moderate and High; 4. Verify counts

Test Scenario: ISSO Toolkit tools match real ATO workflows
Acceptance Criteria: POA&M Risk Acceptance Evidence Inheritance Scheduling Approvals STIG Import Incident Correlation align with ISSO tasks
Steps to Execute: 1. Open each ISSO tool; 2. Verify workflow fit; 3. Confirm output format matches ATO needs

Test Scenario: Automated Vulnerability Management provides actionable remediation
Acceptance Criteria: Attack chains remediation plans validation scripts impact analysis and ticket generation produce useful output
Steps to Execute: 1. Run assessment with failures; 2. Open Vulnerability Management; 3. Use each sub-tool

Test Scenario: SSP narratives use formal RMF language
Acceptance Criteria: Generated SSP control narratives are auditor-ready and use appropriate terminology
Steps to Execute: 1. Run assessment; 2. Generate SSP with AI narratives; 3. Review sample narratives

Test Scenario: User can navigate between all main pages within 3 clicks
Acceptance Criteria: Dashboard Assessment Reports Vulnerability Management SSP Generator AI Assistant Settings and ISSO Toolkit accessible
Steps to Execute: 1. Start from Dashboard; 2. Navigate to each page; 3. Count clicks

Test Scenario: ISSO Toolkit opens in clear hub layout
Acceptance Criteria: Toolkit hub displays; 12 tools visible; navigation between tools intuitive
Steps to Execute: 1. Click ISSO Toolkit; 2. Review hub; 3. Navigate to 3 different tools
Expected Result: Clear hub layout; easy tool access

Test Scenario: Assessment import and manual entry straightforward
Acceptance Criteria: CSV import works; demo data loads; manual assessment UI clear
Steps to Execute: 1. Import CSV; 2. Load demo data; 3. Manually assess a few controls

Test Scenario: AI features triggered with single clear action
Acceptance Criteria: Each AI feature has obvious button; one click invokes; loading state shown
Steps to Execute: 1. Open POA&M; 2. Click AI Generate; 3. Repeat for Risk Acceptance Evidence etc

Test Scenario: Reports and exports usable
Acceptance Criteria: Reports page generates; exports (CSV/JSON) complete; format suitable for downstream use
Steps to Execute: 1. Run assessment; 2. Open Reports; 3. Export in available formats

Test Scenario: Full 200-control assessment runs to completion
Acceptance Criteria: Assessment completes; results persist; findings display correctly by category
Steps to Execute: 1. Run full assessment (CSV or demo); 2. Verify completion; 3. Check Dashboard and Reports

Test Scenario: AI generates POA&M entries for failed findings
Acceptance Criteria: One click produces POA&M entries with milestones due dates and responsible parties
Steps to Execute: 1. Run assessment with failures; 2. Open POA&M Tracker; 3. Click AI Generate POA&M

Test Scenario: AI drafts risk acceptance justification
Acceptance Criteria: AI produces operational justification compensating controls and residual risk assessment
Steps to Execute: 1. Open Risk Acceptance; 2. Select finding; 3. Click AI Draft Justification

Test Scenario: AI analyzes evidence sufficiency
Acceptance Criteria: Per-artifact analysis returns Sufficient/Partial/Insufficient with recommendations
Steps to Execute: 1. Upload evidence; 2. Run Analyze Sufficiency; 3. Review output

Test Scenario: AI classifies control inheritance
Acceptance Criteria: One click classifies controls as Inherited/Common/System-Specific with rationale
Steps to Execute: 1. Open Control Inheritance; 2. Click AI Classify All; 3. Review classifications

Test Scenario: AI maps STIG findings to SOPRA controls
Acceptance Criteria: Imported STIG results map to controls with confidence scores
Steps to Execute: 1. Import STIG/CIS results; 2. Run AI Map; 3. Verify mappings

Test Scenario: AI suggests related controls for incidents
Acceptance Criteria: Incident description yields control correlations with relevance scores
Steps to Execute: 1. Log incident; 2. Run AI Suggest Related; 3. Review suggestions

Test Scenario: ConMon dashboard displays assessment timeline and advisories
Acceptance Criteria: ConMon shows threat level timeline and category advisories
Steps to Execute: 1. Open Continuous Monitoring; 2. Run assessment; 3. View ConMon

Test Scenario: 200-control assessment completes in reasonable time
Acceptance Criteria: Assessment completes in under 5 minutes for demo/import scenarios
Steps to Execute: 1. Run assessment; 2. Record completion time

Test Scenario: AI features respond within acceptable time
Acceptance Criteria: AI generation completes within 60 seconds per operation
Steps to Execute: 1. Trigger AI POA&M generate; 2. Trigger AI Risk draft; 3. Measure response time

Test Scenario: Navigation and page loads responsive
Acceptance Criteria: Page transitions under 5 seconds; no prolonged blank screens
Steps to Execute: 1. Navigate between 5 pages; 2. Note load times

Test Scenario: Output suitable for ATO package
Acceptance Criteria: POA&M Risk Acceptance SSP and Approval workflows produce audit-ready artifacts
Steps to Execute: 1. Generate POA&M Risk Acceptance SSP; 2. Review against ATO checklist

Test Scenario: AI sends only aggregate data no PII or raw evidence
Acceptance Criteria: Verifiable that only assessment metadata sent to Bedrock; no evidence uploads to cloud
Steps to Execute: 1. Use browser DevTools Network tab to inspect outbound requests during AI operations; 2. Confirm only aggregate metadata sent to Bedrock (no raw evidence or PII)
